title: Iridorkis scortechinii
uses: Iridorkis scortechinii, commonly known as the "Bulbous Iris," has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin infections. Its roots and rhizomes are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating colds and flu. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Iridorkis scortechinii for medicinal purposes.

parts: Iridorkis scortechinii, commonly known as the "Sandalwood" or "Yellow Sandalwood", has several medicinal parts. The heartwood, bark, leaves, and roots are all utilized for their medicinal properties. side_effects: Iridorkis scortechinii is known to cause gastrointestinal upset in some individuals, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its emetic properties. The plant is also said to cause allergic reactions in rare cases, such as skin irritation and respiratory issues. In high doses, Iridorkis scortechinii can lead to more severe side effects, including cardiovascular problems and even organ failure. The exact dosage that can cause these side effects is not well-documented, making it essential to handle the plant with caution.
